design_philosophy.core_loop: Execute hockey plays (shoot, pass, check) across multiple periods to outscore opponent; manage line substitutions and goalie deployment strategically
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time positional play and tactical decision-making (when to dump puck, commit checks, change lines) against AI or human opponent
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Manage and play as an NHL team competing for the Stanley Cup across exhibition, season, or playoff series formats
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes authentic hockey mechanics (penalties, line changes, goalie management) and uses Mode 7 3D perspective to keep player focused on puck/center ice rather than arcade-style action. Licensed NHL teams but unlicensed players, with strengths/weaknesses reflecting real counterparts. Offers asymmetric difficulty levels and customizable game length/rule sets rather than forcing single experience.

difficulty.checkpointing: Battery backup allows save progression throughout Stanley Cup playoffs
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three difficulty levels (Junior, NHL, NHL Pro) selectable. Player can customize period length (5, 10, 20 minutes), toggle penalties and line changes on/off, and skip games against particular opponents to tailor challenge and session length

progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through Stanley Cup playoff series (Best of Seven format) or season play; all 26 NHL teams available from start
replayability_hooks: Multiple gameplay modes (Exhibition, Season, Best of Seven)
26 playable teams with different player strength distributions
Customizable game parameters (period length, rules toggle, difficulty)

world_building_techniques: Sportscaster commentary summarizing action at end of each period and after game
Real NHL team branding and lineup structures
Stanley Cup as aspirational goal framework

faction_indicators: 26 distinct NHL teams with identifiable logos and gameplay characteristics
Player strength/weakness assignments mirroring real-life counterparts (e.g., Pittsburgh #66, Los Angeles #99 easier to score with)
danger_signals: Opponent AI difficulty scaling across Junior/NHL/NHL Pro tiers
Playoff series momentum across Best of Seven format
environmental_storytelling: Hockey rink layout and Mode 7 perspective reinforces authentic sport authenticity
Penalty system (tripping, cross-checking, slashing, roughing, hooking, interference, icing, offsides) grounds mechanics in real hockey rules
